text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I didn't win? Are you kidding me?!
choice_data_list: You can aim for first next time!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I didn't win? Are you kidding me?!
choice_data_list: You can aim for first next time!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: So what? It doesn't mean squat if I<n>don't place!
choice_data_list:
You've just gotta play to your strengths, and
you'll be sure to win!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
Isn't the whole reason I lost because<n>I don't have
any strengths?
choice_data_list:
But you do! I can point them out to you while we
watch the replays together.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
No thanks. That sounds like torture.<n>I'll take the
recordings, though.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
(I suppose watching my past races<n>for reference
isn't such a bad idea.)

A few days later, I give Narita Taishin<n>the replays
along with some notes on<n>her running.

She seems annoyed by the notes, but<n>thankfully,
she takes everything, and<n>we get started on our
training<n>session.

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I'm going to win and win and win...

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text:
And I'll rise high above the others so<n>that no
one'll ever talk bad about me!

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: Sigh... You're really getting too firedup about this.

The moment we get back, I look over<n>her training
schedule and talk to her<n>about the next race, but...

text_block_list.name: Narita Taishin
text_block_list.text: I'll win the next one, so calm down<n>already!

Despite being fed up with me, Narita<n>Taishin is
dead set on claiming<n>victory.
